questions.title: Who performed the Nikah khutba (marriage sermon) of Hazrat Muhammad SAW and Hazrat Khadija RA?
questions.explanation:
The Nikah khutba (marriage sermon) of Hazrat Muhammad SAW and Hazrat Khadija RA was performed by Hazrat Abu Talib (RA).

He was the uncle of the Prophet Muhammad SAW.

He was acted as the guardian during the marriage.

questions.title: What was the name of the king of Abyssinia?
questions.explanation:
The king of Abyssinia (modern-day Ethiopia) was Najashi.

He is noted for his favorable reception of early Muslim refugees during the time of the Prophet Muhammad SAW.

questions.title: Name the Uncle of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) who was martyred in the battle of Uhud?
questions.explanation:
Uncle of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) named Hazrat Hamza (RA) was martyred in the battle of Uhad.

Abyssinian slave Wahshi ibn Harb martyred Hazrzat Hamza bin Abdul Mutalib (RA)

Hazrat Hamza Died: on 22 March 625; Mount Uhud, Medina, Arabia

 

 

Additional info

Battle of Uhad was fought in third Hijra in the month of Shawal.

 ****

questions.title: To whom Hazrat Khadija (R.A) took to the Holy Prophet (SAW) after discussing firstly about the prophethood?
questions.explanation:
Khadija (R.A) took the Holy Prophet (SAW) to Warqa bin Nofal after discussing firstly about the prophethood.

Waraqah ibn Nawfal was the cousin Uncle of Hazrat Khadija (RA).

Hazrat Khadija (RA) took him to Waraqah bin Naufal who was a Christian convert and used to read the Gospel in Arabic.

Khadija (RA) was the first wife of the Holy Prophet (PBHU).


Additional Info

Prophethood has been finished on the Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)

The Prophethood is belief in All the Prophets of Allah.

Prophethood is attained by  Bestowed by Allah

***

questions.title: How many days Hazrat Muhammad PBUH stayed during Tabook Battle?
questions.explanation:
• Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) stayed for 20 days in Tabuk
  • The Battle of Tabuk, the last major expedition of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
  • It was fought against the Romans in 9 Hijri (630 AD).
  • It took place in the region of Tabuk during Rajab and Sha'ban.

Last updated by NS on 06 May 2026

questions.title: Which surah is called "Al-Hamd'?
questions.explanation:
• Surah Al-Fatiha is also known as Al-Hamd (The Praise).
    It has 7 verses, 29 words, and 139 letters.
    It is also referred to as:
    • As-Salah (The Prayer)
    • Umm al-Kitab (Mother of the Book)
    • Umm al-Quran (Mother of the Quran)
    • Sab'a min al-Mathani (Seven Repeated Ones)
    • Ash-Shifa' (The Cure).

questions.title: Hazrat Umar (RA) was Caliph for______years.
questions.explanation:
Hazrat Umar ibn al-Khattab was the second caliph of Islam.

He was ruled for 10 years from 634 CE to 644 CE.

He succeeded Hazrat Abu Bakr.

He was instrumental in expanding the Islamic state.

questions.title: اشاروں کنایوں کے الفاظ سے جو طلاق دی جائے کہلا تی ہے؟
questions.explanation:
اشاروں اور کنایات کے الفاظ سے دی جانے والی طلاق کو "طلاق کنائی" یا "طلاق بائن" کہا جاتا ہے۔


طلاق بائن وہ طلاق ہے جو کسی بھی دوبارہ رجوع (تعلق بحال) کے بغیر ایک بار دی جائے۔

طلاق کنائی میں شوہر ایسے الفاظ استعمال کرتا ہے جو براہ راست طلاق کا اعلان نہیں کرتے، بلکہ اشارہ یا کنایہ ہوتا ہے کہ تعلق ختم ہو چکا ہے۔


طلاق بائن کی اقسام


طلاق بائن صغری: جب شوہر پہلی یا دوسری بار طلاق دیتا ہے اور اس کے بعد عدت گزر جاتی ہے، تو یہ طلاق بائن صغری کہلاتی ہے۔


طلاق بائن کبری: جب شوہر تیسری بار طلاق دیتا ہے، تو یہ طلاق بائن کبری کہلاتی ہے اور اس کے بعد شوہر اور بیوی کے درمیان دوبارہ نکاح ممکن نہیں ہوتا جب تک کہ بیوی کسی اور سے نکاح نہ کرے۔


مثالیں:

"تم آزاد ہو"

"اب ہم الگ ہو چکے ہیں"

"تم میرے لیے نہیں ہو"

questions.title: پہلا جنگی بحری بیڑا کس نے تیار کیا تھا؟
questions.explanation:
حضرت عثمان بن عفان نے اسلامی سلطنت میں پہلا جنگی بحری بیڑا تیار کیا۔

یہ بیڑا 29 ہجری میں تیار کیا گیا تھا اور اس کا مقصد بحری جنگی کی صلاحیت کو بہتر بنانا تھا۔

questions.title: تالمود کس مزہب کی کتاب ہے؟
questions.explanation:
تالمود یہودیت کی مقدس کتاب ہے۔ یہ کتاب یسراٰئیلی (یہودی) قوانین، روایات، اور فلسفے کا مجموعہ ہے۔

تالمود دو بنیادی حصوں پر مشتمل ہے

میشنا: یہ حصہ ابتدائی یہودی قوانین اور احکام پر مشتمل ہے، جو پہلے صدیوں میں تحریر کیے گئے۔

گیمارا: یہ حصہ مشنا کی تشریحات اور تبصرے پر مشتمل ہے، جو بعد کے صدیوں میں تحریر کیا گیا۔

questions.title: Where the torait was revealed on Hazrat Musa (AS)?
questions.explanation:
The Torah was revealed to Hazrat Musa (AS) at Koh-e-Toor (Mount Sinai).

It comprises the five books of Musa.

It known in Hebrew as Chameesha Choomshey Torah.

The word 'Torait' appears 18 times in the Quran.

Hazrat Musa's name is mentioned 136 times.

The four Holy Books are the:

  1. Torah (Musa)
  2. Zabur (Dawud)
  3. Injil (Isa)
  4. Quran (Muhammad).

questions.title: Who among the companion (Sahaba) listened to the whole of the Quran from the Prophet Muhammad (SAW)?
questions.explanation:
Abdullah bin Masud was the companion who listened to the entire Quran from Prophet Muhammad (SAW).

He was a renowned scholar and memorizer of the Quran among the Sahaba.

questions.title: The place chosen to sacrifice Hazrat Ismail (AS) was ____?
questions.explanation:
The place chosen to sacrifice Hazrat Ismail (AS) was Mina.

This site, near Mecca, is integral to the Hajj rituals.

Allah (SWT) provided a ram to be sacrificed instead of Ismail (AS).

questions.title: Which prophet was tilted as 'Zabeeh Ullah' (Zabiullah)?
questions.explanation:
• Hazrat Ismail (AS) is called 'Zabeeh Ullah' (Zabiullah).
    • Hazrat Ismail (A.S) was Son of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) & Hazrat Hajra (A.S).
    • Prophet Ismail (AS) and Prophet Ishaq (AS) are Brothers.

questions.title: Aitekaaf (Seclusion) during the month of Ramzan is ____?
questions.explanation:
• Aitekaaf (seclusion) during Ramadan is considered Sunnah Muakkadah, an emphasized Sunnah.
  • While not obligatory for every Muslim.
  • It is highly recommended and carries significant spiritual benefits.

questions.title: Which article of the constitution of 1973 is related to the affairs of Zakat?
questions.explanation:
Article 31 of the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan deals with Zakat.

It establishing a system to support the welfare of the needy.

questions.title: Jizya means _____?
questions.explanation:
• Jizya is a poll tax historically imposed on non-Muslims in Muslim states.
    • In India, Qutb-ud-din Aibak was the first to impose Jizya.
    • The tax was abolished by Mughal ruler Akbar in the 16th century but reintroduced by Aurangzeb in the 17th century.
    • Kharaj was a land tax imposed on non-Muslim cultivators and landlords.

questions.title: المساکین کس ام المومنین کا لقب ہے ؟
questions.answer: حضرت زینب بنت خزیمہ رضی اللہ عنہا
questions.explanation:
ام المساکین حضرت زینب بنت خزیمہ رضی اللہ عنہا کا لقب ہے۔

ان کا یہ لقب ان کی سخاوت اور غریبوں اور مسکینوں کی مدد کی وجہ سے مشہور ہوا

حضرت زینب بنت خزیمہ رضی اللہ عنہا کو "ام المساکین" کا لقب ان کے زمانے کے لوگوں نے دیا تھا۔

questions.title: On 1st Shawwal, 8th Hijra a strong Islamic force approached the valley of Hunain?
questions.explanation:
On 1st Shawwal, 8th Hijra (630 CE), a force of 12,000 Muslims approached the valley of Hunain.

The Battle of Hunain, fought near al-Ta'if.

The Battle of Hunain resulted in a Muslim victory over the Bedouin tribes.

questions.title: The nation of _______ was exterminated through the scourge of floods.
questions.explanation:
The nation of Hazrat Nooh (A.S) was exterminated through the scourge of floods.

The flood is also known as Tofaan-e-Noah.

It was a divine punishment for their disbelief and corruption.

The duration of the Toofan-e-Noah (Flood of Noah) was around 6 months.

The event occurred 2,242 years after Hazrat Adam (AS).

About 80 people were with him in the boat.

The boat of Hazrat Noah (A.S) is referred to as the Ark.

Hazrat Noah (A.S) and those with him remained in the Ark for about six months.

After the floodwaters receded, the Ark is believed to have come to rest on Mount Judi in Turkey.

The nation of Noah worshiped five idols:

  1. Wadh
  2. Suwa
  3. Yaghuth
  4. Yauq
  5. Nas

Hazrat Noah (AS) lived for 950 years.

He was sent to Iraq.

He is called the predecessor, Naji Ullah, and Shaikh ul Ambiya.

His title is Abu ul Bashr Sani.

After the flood, the city of Khasran was established.

questions.title: Adam is a word of which language?
questions.explanation:
The height of Hazrat Adam (AS) was 90 feet.

Adam is a word of Herbew language.

Hebrew is official language in Israel.

He was the first Muslim, as the Quran states that all the Prophets preached the same faith of Islam.

Known for being the first human being.

His spouse was Hawwā (حواء).

Hazrat Adam (AS) was 130 years old when Shees (AS) was born.

He spoke the language in the Garden of Eden.

His total age was 930 years.

Hazrat Adam (AS) met with the Holy Prophet ﷺ on the 1st heaven.

He had 3 sons.

His grave is located in Sri Lanka.

questions.title: The Grand Mosque at Damascus is located in ____?
questions.explanation:
The Great Mosque of Damascus is also known as the Umayyad Mosque.

It was built by the Umayyad Caliph al-Walīd I between 705 and 715 CE.

It is located in the old city of Damascus.

It is the capital of Syria.

It is one of the largest and oldest mosques in the world.

The mosque opened in 715 AD.

questions.title: Which one out of the following is a renowned biographer of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?
questions.explanation:
• Michael H. Hart, renowned for his biography of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).
  • He authored "The 100: A Ranking of the Most Influential Persons in History," first published in 1978.
  • In this book, Muhammad (PBUH) was ranked as the most influential person in history, ahead of Jesus or Moses.
  • The book has two editions: the first in 1978 and the second in 1992.

questions.title: How many vowels does Arabic Language have?
questions.explanation:
The Arabic language has three vowels: /a/, /i/, and /u/.

These vowels, which can be short or long, affect the pronunciation and meaning of words.

Vowels were inserted in the Quran in 43 Hijri.

questions.title: Fulfillment of valid needs causes ____?
questions.explanation:
"Fulfillment of valid needs causes satisfaction."

Meeting genuine needs leads to a sense of contentment and well-being.

questions.title: You are in the parking lot of a grocery store. You notice a couple loading their groceries into their car. You see them getting into their car, leaving a bottle of 10 liters of cooking oil on the bottom of the trolley. What will you do?
questions.answer: Approach the car before they pull away and tell them that they left their oil in the trolley
questions.explanation:
"Approach the car before they pull away and tell them that they left their oil in the trolley" is the best action.

This response is ethical and considerate.

It ensuring the couple retrieves their forgotten item.

questions.title: Which of the following action depicts the "keeping of principle"?
questions.answer: Doing such action which is ethically necessary to do
questions.explanation:
"Doing such action which is ethically necessary to do" depicts the "keeping of principle."

This means adhering to ethical standards and principles even without external enforcement or supervision.

questions.title: Laws of nature are different from laws of ethics in which regard?
questions.answer: Laws of nature cannot be broken
questions.explanation:
"Laws of nature cannot be broken" distinguishes them from laws of ethics.

Natural laws describe consistent, unbreakable phenomena in the physical world.

Whereas ethical laws can be violated by human actions.

questions.title: How can one become a good human being?
questions.explanation:
"By following God's order" is how one can become a good human being.

Aligning actions with divine guidance often encourages moral and ethical behavior.

It contributing to personal and communal well-being.

questions.title: What is one's good ethical character?
questions.answer: To do good because it's a right action
questions.explanation:
"To do good because it's a right action" reflects one's good ethical character.

Acting rightly based on moral principles.

questions.title: All regions resemble resembles in which basic point?
questions.explanation:
All regions and religions resemble each other in the belief in the Oneness of God.

This fundamental concept transcends geographical and cultural boundaries.

It emphasizing the unity and singularity of the divine.

questions.title: Which of the following sajdah is performed at the end of a prayer if a mistake is made while performing a prayer?
questions.explanation:
"Sajda-e-Sahw" is the sajdah performed at the end of a prayer if a mistake is made while performing a prayer.

This prostration is done to compensate for errors or omissions in the prayer.

questions.title: What is the standard of respect in Islam?
questions.explanation:
The standard of respect in Islam is piety.

In Islam, respect and honor are based on an individual's piety and righteousness.

questions.title: The best way of living is ____?
questions.explanation:
Moderation suggests a balanced and reasonable approach to life.

It avoiding extremes and excesses.

questions.title: What is called that place where Hazrat Ibrahim (AS) stood while constructing the Kaaba?
questions.explanation:
• Maqam Ibrahim is the place where Hazrat Ibrahim (AS) stood while constructing the Kaaba.
  • It is located near the Kaaba in Makkah.
  • It is sacred and associated with the footprint of Prophet Ibrahim (AS).

questions.title: Who was the commanders of the Infidels in the Battle of Uhud?
questions.explanation:
• The Battle of Uhud occurred on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD (3 Hijri) in the valley north of Mount Uhud.
    • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) led around 1000 Muslims in the Battle of Uhud.
    • They faced 3000 Qurayshi Meccans commanded by Abu Sufyan ibn Harb.
    • Abdullah bin Jubair was appointed by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) to lead the 50 skilled archers positioned on Uhud Mountain.

questions.title: Abu-al-Hikam is the title of?
questions.explanation:
• Abu Jahal, commonly known as Abu Hakam (“Father of Wisdom”) among the Quraysh, was considered a wise man.
    • He was fatally wounded in the Battle of Badr by Mu'adh ibn 'Afrā' and Mu'ādh ibn 'Amr.
    • He was finally killed by Abdullah ibn Masud.
    • He was born in 570 A.D.
    • Abu Jahal died by beheading at the Battle of Badr.
    • He was one of the Meccan polytheist pagan leaders from the Quraysh.

questions.title: When the first migration to Abyssinia took place?
questions.explanation:
Surah Anfal mentions the migration of the Holy Prophet SAW.

The first migration to Abyssinia (Habshah) took place in 613 A.D.

Including Prophet Muhammad's relatives Usman Ghani (R.A) and Ruqaiyah (R.A).

This group comprised 12 men and 4 women.

The second migration to Abyssinia occurred in 616 A.D.

This group comprised 83 men and 18 to 19 women.

questions.title: The comb was invented by Prophet?
questions.explanation:
Hazrat Ibrahim (AS) is a prominent example of faith in Judaism, Christianity, and Islam.

He is also credited with inventing the comb.

questions.title: What was the name of town in Israel which was the home of Hazrat Issa (AS) in his youth?
questions.explanation:
• The town in Israel that was the home of Hazrat Issa (AS) in his youth is Nazareth.
    • It is known as the place where Jesus (AS) grew up.

questions.title: Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A) belonged to the tribe of _____?
questions.explanation:
• Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) was born on 27 October 573 CE in Mecca to a wealthy family of the Banu Taym tribe of the Quraysh.
    • His father, Uthman, was given the laqab Abu Quhafa, and his mother, Salma bint Sakhar, was given the laqab Umm ul-Khair.
    • He had sons named Abdullah ibn Abi Bakr and Abdul-Rah.

questions.title: Which Sahabi was known as Brain of Arab?
questions.explanation:
• The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave the title Brain of Arab to Hazrat Amr bin al-Aas R.A.
  • Hazrat Amr bin Al-Aas was the Arab commander.
  • He led the Muslim conquest of Egypt.
  • He served as Egypt's governor in 640–646 and 658–664.
  • Hazrat Amr bin Al-Aas embraced Islam in 629 AD.
  • He was Born: 573 AD Mecca, Hejaz.
  • He was Died: 664 AD (aged 90–91) Egypt, Umayyad Caliphate.

questions.title: During Which prayer, the change of Qibla happened?
questions.explanation:
The change of Qibla from Jerusalem to the Kaaba in Mecca occurred during the Zuhr (midday) prayer in 624 CE.

This significant event took place in the second (2nd) year of the Hijra.

questions.title: Bird Hudhud (Woodpecker) is mentioned in which Surah?
questions.explanation:
The bird Hudhud (hoopoe) appears twice in Surah An-Naml (27th chapter) of the Quran.

It played a crucial role in carrying messages between Prophet Sulayman (AS) and the Queen of Sheba.

questions.title: How many Umahaat ul Momineen were Hufaaz (memorizers of the Quran)?
questions.explanation:
Three Umahaat ul Momineen (Mothers of the Believers) were Hufaaz (memorizers of the Quran):

  1. Hazrat Aisha (RA)
  2. Hazrat Hafsa (RA)
  3. Hazrat Umm Salama (RA)

They were known for their deep knowledge and preservation of the Quranic verses.

questions.title: Qaroon was a treasurer, he was in the reign of which Prophet?
questions.explanation:
Qaroon was a treasurer during the reign of Prophet Musa (AS).

He is often remembered for his immense wealth and arrogance.

This ultimately led to his downfall as recounted in the Quran.

questions.title: In which Islamic Battle Angeles helped the Muslims?
questions.explanation:
• The Battle of Badr is also known as The Day of the Criterion.
    • Day of the Criterion means کسوٹی کا دن
    • It took place on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH) near the city of Badr.
    • Prophet Muhammad SAW, commanding his Sahaba, defeated the Quraysh army led by Abu Jahl (Amr ibn Hishām).
    • The Kufar forces numbered 1,000. Fourteen Muslims were killed, while 70 Kufar were killed and 70 captured.
    • Surah Al-Anfal mentions that Allah promised to reinforce the Muslims with 1,000 angels.

questions.title: Which Surah of the following Quran is known as "Surah of Patience"?
questions.explanation:
Surah Yusuf is known as the "Surah of Patience" in the Quran.

It narrates the story of Prophet Joseph (Yusuf).

It highlighting patience as a central theme throughout his trials and tribulations.

questions.title: Which Pious Caliph had to fight against those who refuse to pay zakat?
questions.explanation:
• Hazrat Abu Bakr, the first Caliph, engaged in Jihad over the issue of Zakat.
  • During the Ridda Wars in July 632, he dispatched envoys to rebellious tribes demanding payment of Zakat, but they refused.

questions.title: Torat (Torah) means ________?
questions.explanation:
"Torah" means "Law" in Hebrew.

The Torah has a range of meanings.

It refers to the first five books of the Hebrew Bible.

It is also known as the Five Books of Moses or Chameesha Choomshey Torah in Hebrew.

It encompasses the laws and teachings given to the Israelites.

The Torah contains 187 chapters and 5,852 verses.

questions.title: Where is the grave of Hazrat Noah (AS)?
questions.explanation:
Grave of Hazrat Noah (AS) is situated in Jordan.

Hazrat Noah saved life at the mythic enormous flood on the earth.

questions.title: No of Kufars (Non Muslims Forces) in Battle of Badr were _______?
questions.explanation:
• "Badr" means "full moon" in Arabic.
  • The Battle of Badr, also called Furqan.
  • The Battle of Badr is also known as The Day of the Criterion.
  • Day of the Criterion means کسوٹی کا دن
  • It took place on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH) near the city of Badr.
  • Prophet Muhammad SAW, commanding his Sahaba, defeated the Quraysh army led by Abu Jahl (Amr ibn Hishām).
  • The Kufar forces numbered 1,000.
  • It was the first battle of Islam, with 313-317 Muslims.
  • The Muslim forces included 82-85 Muhajirun and 231 Ansar (61 from Aws and 170 from Khazraj).
  • They had 2 horses and 70 camels.
  • Fourteen Muslims were killed, while 70 Kufar were killed and 70 captured.
  • Surah Al-Anfal mentions that Allah promised to reinforce the Muslims with 1,000 angels.

questions.title: How many disciples of Hazrat Essa (AS) assisted him in preaching?
questions.explanation:
Twelve companions assisted Hazrat Isa (AS).

He was born in Bethlehem.

He is known as Rohullah.

The Injeel was revealed to him.

questions.title: In which Surah of the Holy Quran, the regulations for prisoners of war mentioned?
questions.explanation:
• Surah An-Nisa (Chapter 4) of the Holy Quran contains regulations for various aspects of life, including the treatment of prisoners of war (Verse 4:76).
  • It emphasizes treating prisoners with kindness and respect, and provides guidance on their release and ransom.
  • This Surah also focuses on social justice, women's rights, and compassion.
  • Quran 76:8 supports the position that prisoners must be fed in a dignified manner and not forced to beg.

questions.title: When did Ottoman Sultan Muhammad II Fateh capture the Roman Empire Capital, Constantinople?
questions.explanation:
Constantinople قسطنطنیہ

Ottoman Sultan Muhammad II Fateh captured Constantinople on May 29, 1453.

This marking the end of the Byzantine Empire.

It establishing Ottoman control over the city.

questions.title: Fatwa Kazi Khan is the authentic fatwa of which sect?
questions.explanation:
"Fatwa Kazi Khan" is an authoritative fatwa collection of the Fiqah Hanfia (Hanafi) sect.

The Fatwa-e-Qazi Khan consists of two volumes.

It containing 500 verses from the Holy Qur'an and 290 Traditions of the Holy Prophet SAW.

It covering marriage, dower, divorce, legitimacy, and guardianship of minors.

questions.title: Which was the first institution of Islam?
questions.explanation:
The Suffah was originally in the northeast corner of the Mosque.

When Muhammad SAW was ordered by Allah to change the Qibla towards Mecca in the south.

The Suffah was left at the rear of the mosque, where it remained.

questions.title: In Masjid-e-Aqsa the word Aqsa means _____?
questions.explanation:
• In Masjid-e-Aqsa, the word Aqsa means "Far away" or "the remote".
  • Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) first laid the foundation.
  • Al-Masjid Al-Aqsa was the first qibla in Islam.
  • It was built 40 years after Al-Masjid Al-Haram in Makkah.

Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026

questions.title: What is the meaning of Quranic Surah Al-Nas?
questions.explanation:
The last chapter (sūrah) of the Quran.

It is named "An-Nas," which means "people" or "mankind."

questions.title: Name the Surah in Ahl-E-Kitab and Munafqeen are mentioned.
questions.explanation:
•  In Makki Ahl-E-Kitab and Munafqeen are mentioned
  • ***
  • Ahl-E-Kitab and Munafqeen are mentioned in Surah Munafqun
  • Surah Munafiqun is the 63 Surah of the Quran and consists of 11 verses.
  • **
  • Christians and Jews are referred to as Ahl al-Kitab,
  • Jesus alone is mentioned in the Qur'an more than 30 times.

questions.title: How many Rukus are there in Surah Al-Baqrah?
questions.explanation:
• Al-Baqarah (The Cow or The Heifer) is the second and longest chapter of the Quran, with 286 verses.
  • It derives its name from the story of the cow (vv. 67-73).
  • Verse 282, the longest verse, is known as 'āyatu d-dayn.
  • It consists of 25,500 letters and is positioned in Juzʼ 1–3.
  • The chapter has 40 Rukus and is classified as Medinan.
  • Hazrat Ibrahim (علیہ السلام) is mentioned 13 times.
  • The verse 183 describes the obligation of fasting (soam).

questions.title: In which Surah of Quran mentioned that the number of months are Twelve?
questions.explanation:

questions.title: How many Angels are mentioned in Holy Quran?
questions.explanation:
There are 7 Angels are mentioned in Holy Quran.

1. Gabriel (upon whom be peace):
Gabriel is the angel whose task is to convey the revelation from Allah to His Messengers.

2. Mikaa’eel (upon whom be peace):
Mikaa’eel is the angel responsible for distribution of sustenance wherever Allah wishes.

3. Israafeel (upon whom be peace):
Israfeel (a.s.) is the angel who is charged with blowing the trumpet on the Day of Judgment.

4. Azraeel (upon whom be peace):
He is the Angel of death to take the souls with the order of Allah swt. He is mentioned in the hadiths and ayahs as “Angel of Death.”

5. Kiram al Katibin:
“Keepers” mentioned in the ayahs (Surah al Infitar; 10-12) and (Surah al An’am, 61) are the “kiram al katibin” angels that are the two angels recording the deeds.

6. Hafaza (The Guardian Angels)
“Witnesses” are the hafaza angels who are charged with guarding.

7. Hamalat al-‘Arsh:
They are the angels carrying the arsh (throne).

questions.title: Sam, Ham, and Yapheth were sons of which prophet?
questions.explanation: Sam, Ham, and Yapheth were sons of Hazrat Noah (AS).

questions.title: Who built Darulandah?
questions.explanation: Darulandah was built by Qasi bin Kalab.

questions.title: Who was the first to breastfeed the Holy Prophet (PBUH) among the maidservants?
questions.explanation: Hazrat Sobia (RA) was the first maidservant to breastfeed the Holy Prophet (PBUH) after his birth.

questions.title: Who is the author of "The Declaration of Signatories"?
questions.explanation:
• "The Declaration of Signatories" (Al-'Aqidah Al-Waasitiyah) is a famous book written by Imam Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyya (1292-1350 CE), a renowned Islamic scholar and theologian.
  • The book is a concise and authoritative exposition of Islamic creed and doctrine.

questions.title: On the occasion of Hajj in 9 Hijri, which companion was sent to Makkah by the Holy Prophet (PBUH) as a messenger?
questions.explanation: On the occasion of Hajj in 9 Hijri, Hazrat Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA) was sent to Makkah by the Holy Prophet (PBUH) as a messenger.

questions.title: Whose testimony was considered equal to that of two men among the companions?
questions.explanation: Hazrat Khuzaima ibn Thabit (RA) had his testimony considered equal to that of two men.

questions.title: How many copies of the Quran did Shah Wali Allah write?
questions.explanation:
• Shah Wali Allah wrote three handwritten copies of the Quran, which are considered priceless treasures of Islamic calligraphy and scholarship.
  • One copy is preserved in the National Archives of India, while the other two are in private collections.

questions.title: Ghazwa Banu Qurayzah was fought immediately after which battle?
questions.explanation: Ghazwa Banu Qurayzah was fought immediately after the Battle of Khandaq (Trench).

questions.title: In which Surah of the Holy Quran are women commanded to cover themselves?
questions.explanation: Women are commanded to cover themselves in Surah An-Nur, verse 31.

questions.title: Who did the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) send a letter to on the occasion of Hajj in 9 Hijri?
questions.explanation: In 9 Hijri, the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) sent letters to the rulers of neighboring countries, including the Roman Emperor Heraclius, the Persian Emperor Khosrau, and others, inviting them to accept Islam and warning them of the consequences of rejecting the truth.

questions.title: Who was given the title Zaat un Nataqin?
questions.explanation:
Hazrat Asma (RA) was given the title Zaat un Nataqin.

Which means "She of the Two Belts"

Her nickname was given to her by Muhammad during the migration to Medina

She prepared food for this journey.

She tied the food on the camel's back with her belt, as nothing else could be found


She was Abu Bakr's daughter.

Her mother was Qutaylah bint Abd al-Uzza

She was half-sisters were Aisha and Umm Kulthum bint Abi Bakr,

questions.title: In which Surah is the mention of Hajj, Fasting etc?
questions.explanation: The mention of Hajj, fasting, and other religious obligations is found in: Surah Al-Baqarah.

questions.title: When did Hazrat Imam Tirmazy die?
questions.explanation: Muhammad ibn Isa al-Tirmidhi, often referred to as Imām at-Termezī or Tirmidhī, was an Islamic scholar who died on October 9, 892 CE.

questions.title: How many words exist in Quran Majeed
questions.explanation:
The Quran Majeed contains approximately 77,432 words.

The number of verses is generally agreed to be 6,236, excluding the Bismillah, or 6,348

questions.title: Before invading Sindh, bin Qasim was the governor of?
questions.explanation: Before invading Sindh, bin Qasim was the governor of Fars.

questions.title: Who levied taxes on new converts to Islam?
questions.explanation:
Jizya tax was imposed on Non-Muslims during the Holy Prophet's (PBUH) life

But it was formalized during Caliph Umar bin Khatab.

questions.title: Mualim Thani is also known as?
questions.explanation: Mualim Thani is also known as Minhaj Siraj.

questions.title: When was the Battle of Nahrawan fought?
questions.explanation:
The Battle of Nahrawan was fought on July 17, 658 AD (Safar 38 AH).

The Battle of Nahrawan was fought between Hazrat Ali (RA) and the Kharjis.

questions.title: Who wrote the book "Taqat e Nasri"?
questions.explanation: "Taqat e Nasri" was written by Minhaj Siraj.

questions.title: Which false prophet was killed during the life of Holy Prophet Muhammad (SAW)?
questions.explanation:
Musaylimah was a false prophet who claimed prophethood during the lifetime of HolyProphet Muhammad (SAW)

He was killed in the Battle of Yamama in 632 CE.

Musailama al Kazzab False Prophet Was Called The Liar’ By The Holy Prophet 

Wahshi ibn Harb killed Muslema Kazab (Musailamah al Kazzab)

questions.title: What was the approximate year when the Umayyad rule in Spain ended?
questions.explanation: The Umayyad rule in Spain ended in 1031 CE.

questions.title: When did the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) perform Umrah after the Treaty of Hudaybiyah?
questions.explanation:
• The Treaty of Hudaybiyah was signed in 628 CE, and the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) performed Umrah in 629 CE, the following year.
  • This was part of the agreement reached in the treaty, which allowed the Muslims to perform Umrah in Mecca.

questions.title: Who was the Persian general in the Battle of al-Qādisiyyah?
questions.explanation: The Persian general in the Battle of al-Qādisiyyah was Rostam Farrokhzād.

questions.title: What was the Expedition of Usra?
questions.answer: A military expedition led by the Prophet Muhammad (SAW)
questions.explanation:
The Expedition of Usra, also known as the Expedition of Usra ibn al-Huqaq.


Here are some key points about the Expedition of Usra:

  • Date: 6th Hijri year (627 CE)
  • Leader: Prophet Muhammad (SAW)
  • Purpose: To defend against a potential threat from the tribe of Usra
  • Outcome: Successful defeat of the tribe
  • Location: Usra (a place near Medina)

questions.title: Which Surah was revealed after the Battle of Badr?
questions.explanation: Surah Al-Anfal: This Surah was revealed after the Battle of Badr and contains verses that relate to the battle, including 8:5-19, 8:42-48, 8:70-71.

questions.title: In which battle did the Prophet SAW appoint three generals?
questions.explanation: The Prophet SAW appointed Khalid ibn al-Walid, Abu Ubaidah ibn al-Jarrah and Abu Amr ibn al-As as generals in the Battle of Hunain.

questions.title: What was the number of main clans of the Quraish tribe?
questions.explanation:
There are 10 main clans of the Quraish tribe:

  1. Hāshim, the clan of the Prophet himself
  2. Zuhra, the clan of the Prophet's mother
  3. Taim, the clan of the first caliph, Abū Bakr
  4. ʿAdī, the clan of the second caliph, ʿUmar I
  5. Umayya, the clan of the third caliph, ʿUthmān, and his relatives, the dynasty of the Umayyad caliphs
  6. Banu al-Harith
  7. Banu Lu'ayy ibn Ghalib
  8. Banu 'Amir
  9. Banu Ka'b
  10. Banu Adi

questions.title: What was the name of the leader of the non-Muslim forces during the Battle of Hunain?
questions.explanation: Malik bin ‘Awf was the supreme commander of the Hawazin tribe, which allied with the Bani Thaqif to fight against the Muslims in the Battle of Hunain.

questions.title: What was the father's name of Bibi Halima (RA), the wet nurse of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)?
questions.explanation:
• Bibi Halima (RA) was the wet nurse of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and her father's name was Harith bin Abdul Uzza.
  • She played an important role in the early life of the Prophet (PBUH) and is considered a respected figure in Islamic history.

questions.title: How many Ghazwa was fought in the month of Ramadan?
questions.explanation:
The four Ghazwa fought in the month of Ramadan are:

  1. Ghazwa Badr
  2. Ghazwa Buwat
  3. Ghazwa Khandak
  4. Ghazwa Hamra-al-Asad

questions.title: Hazrat Khadija (R.A) bore all the children of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) except?
questions.explanation: The children of Hazrat Muhammad SAW are said to have been born to his first wife Khadija bint Khuwaylid, except his son Ibrahim, who was born to Maria al-Qibtiyya.

questions.title: The Ghazwa-e-Badr is also known as?
questions.explanation:
• Ghazwa-e-Badr is also known as Ghazwa-e-Kubra, which means "The Great Battle of Badr".
  • This battle took place on 17th Ramadan, 2 AH (624 CE) and was a decisive victory for the Muslims led by the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) against the Meccan polytheists.

questions.title: Which tribes accepted Islam after the Ghazwa-e-Mota?
questions.explanation: Banu Aos and Khazraj embraced Islam after Ghazwa-e-Mota.

questions.title: Which surah of the Quran is named after cow?
questions.explanation:
Surah Bakra is named after a cow. "The Heifer" or "The Cow"

It has been named after the story of the Cow  (vv. 67-73).

It is the second and longest chapter of the Quran.

282 verse is the longest in this surah is known as ayatu d-dayn

Position: Juz 1–3

No. of Rukus: 40

They consist of 25,500 letters



Additional information:

There are six chapters of the Quran are named after animals or insects:

Surat 2, Al Baqarah (The Cow)

Surat 6, Al Anaam (The Cattle)

Surat 16, Al Nahl (The Bees)

Surat 27, Al Naml (The Ants)

Surat 29, Al Ankabut (The Spider)

Surat 105, Al-Fil (The Elephant).

questions.title: Which Muslim pious caliph conquered Iran?
questions.explanation:
Umar was the second Muslim caliph (from 634),

Under whom Arab armies conquered Mesopotamia and Syria

And began the conquest of Iran and Egypt.


Additional information:

The Pious Caliphs were the first four Caliphs.

They were:

questions.title: قرآن میں سب سے زیادہ زکر کس پیغمبر کا آیا ہے؟
questions.explanation:
قرآن مجید میں سب سے زیادہ ذکر حضرت موسیٰ (علیہ السلام) کا آیا ہے


۔ حضرت موسیٰ کی کہانی اور ان کے واقعات قرآن میں مختلف سورۃٰں اور آیات میں تفصیل سے بیان ہوئے ہیں


، اور ان کا ذکر 136 بار سے زیادہ آیا ہے۔ یہ ذکر مختلف مواقع پر آیا ہے،


جیسے کہ ان کی نبوت، فرعون کے ساتھ ان کا مقابلہ، اور بنی اسرائیل کو مصر سے نکالنا وغیرہ۔

questions.title: What is the name of the Holy book of Sikhism?
questions.explanation:
The Guru Granth Sahib is the name for the holy book of Sikhs.

It contains 5867 sacred hymns or Shabads. 
It is made up of 1430 pages 
The language of the holy book is Gurmukhi
‘Ik Onkar’ is the first line of the holy book of Sikh which means ‘there is only one god’.

questions.title: Najashi belong to which country?
questions.explanation:
Najashi belong to Negash, Kingdom of Axum (present-day Ethiopia)

He is a Muslim Boy Name.

He gave shelter to Muslim emigrants from Mecca, around 615–616 at Axum

H3 reigned for almost 17 years from 614–630 CE

questions.title: Nation of Hazrat Hood (AS) destroyed by?
questions.explanation: The nation of Prophet Hood (A.S) was destroyed by continuous rain and storm after seven days.

questions.title: Who may rightly be called the saviour of Islam?
questions.explanation:
Hazrat Abu Bakr is called the Saviour of Islam

because he saved Islam After the death of Prophet Muhammad,

He was the closest companion of the holy Prophet.

The holy Prophet called him Al-Siddiq. 


Additional information:


Abu Bakr's kunniyat was  Abu Abdullah 

He was the father-in-law of the Islamic prophet Muhammad

His daughter Aisha married with prophet Muhammad

His real name was Abdullah Ibn Abi Quhafa.

First Caliph Hazrat Abu Bakr Siddiq (R.A)

He belonged to the Tribe: Quraysh (Banu Taym)

His father's name is Uthman Abu Quhafa.

His Mother's name is Salma Ummul-Khair.

Place of burial: Al Masjid an Nabawi, Al-Madinah al-Munawwarah, Saudi Arabia

questions.title: Giving salaries to the scholars and teachers was started in the time of Caliph?
questions.explanation:
Giving salaries to scholars and teachers was started during the time of Caliph Umar (RA).

Hazrat Umar ibn al-Khattab was the second caliph of Islam.

He was ruled for 10 years from 634 CE to 644 CE.

He succeeded Hazrat Abu Bakr.

He was instrumental in expanding the Islamic state.

questions.title: What is the significance of Eid-ul-Fitr in Islam?
questions.answer: Marking the end of the Ramadan and the fasting period

questions.title: امام احمد کی کتاب کا کیا نام ہے؟
questions.explanation:
Ahmad ibn Hanbal was a Sunni Muslim scholar, jurist, theologian, traditionist, ascetic.
Eponym of the Hanbali school of Islamic jurisprudence
ne of the four major orthodox legal schools of Sunni Islam. 
Born: November 780 AD,  Iraq
Died: August 2, 855 AD Iraq

questions.title: Who is the current Religious Affairs minister of KPK?
questions.explanation:
Muhammad Adnan Qadri  is the current Religious Affairs minister of Khyberpahthtun Khwa (KPK)

Appointed in March 2024.

questions.title: Who is the current Communication & Works minister of KPK?
questions.explanation:
Shakeel Ahmad is the current Communication & Works minister of Khyberpahthtun Khwa (KPK) 

Appointed in March 2024.

questions.title: The principle of non-maleficence primarily focuses on?
questions.explanation:
The principle of non-maleficence primarily focuses on avoiding harm to others.

It emphasizes the importance of preventing harm and minimizing risks to individuals.

questions.title: In the context of medical ethics, informed consent is primarily associated with which ethical principle?
questions.explanation:
In the context of medical ethics, informed consent is primarily associated with the ethical principle of autonomy.

It emphasizes the right of individuals to make informed decisions about their own medical treatment, free from coercion or undue influence.

questions.title: According to utilitarianism, an action is morally right if it?
questions.answer: Produces the greatest good for the greatest number
questions.explanation: According to utilitarianism, an action is morally right if it produces the greatest good for the greatest number of people.

questions.title: What is the primary focus of virtue ethics?
questions.explanation:
The primary focus of virtue ethics is the development of moral character.

It emphasizes cultivating virtuous traits and habits to lead a morally good life, rather than focusing solely on the consequences of actions, moral duties and obligations, or the maximization of happiness.

questions.title: Which ethical principle advocates treating all individuals with equal respect and dignity?
questions.explanation: The ethical principle that advocates treating all individuals with equal respect and dignity is the Principle of justice.

questions.title: The concept of "justice as fairness" is associated with which ethical philosopher?
questions.explanation:
The concept of "justice as fairness" is associated with the ethical philosopher John Rawls.

He developed this idea in his influential work "A Theory of Justice," where he proposed principles of justice based on the notion of fairness, particularly through the veil of ignorance.

questions.title: According to Kantian ethics, an action is morally right if?
questions.answer: It is performed out of duty and follows the categorical imperative
questions.explanation:
According to Kantian ethics, an action is morally right if it is performed out of duty and follows the categorical imperative.

It emphasizes the importance of acting in accordance with universal principles or maxims, regardless of the consequences or personal desires.

questions.title: Which ethical theory prioritizes the intrinsic value of actions rather than their consequences?
questions.explanation:
Deontology prioritizes the intrinsic value of actions rather than their consequences.

It judges the morality of actions based on principles, duties, or rules, regardless of the outcomes or consequences.

questions.title: Which ethical theory emphasizes the importance of one's intentions in determining the morality of an action?
questions.explanation:
Deontology emphasizes the importance of one's intentions in determining the morality of an action.

It focuses on the inherent rightness or wrongness of actions based on principles or rules, rather than solely on the consequences of those actions.

questions.title: Which ethical theory emphasizes the importance of the consequences of actions?
questions.explanation:
Utilitarianism emphasizes the importance of the consequences of actions.

It evaluates the morality of an action based on its ability to produce the greatest overall happiness or well-being for the greatest number of people.

questions.title: The theory which not justify capital punishment is?
questions.explanation: The theory which does not justify capital punishment is the Reformative theory.

questions.title: The right which justify the Capital punishment is?
questions.explanation: The right which justifies Capital punishment is the Right to Live.

questions.title: ‘Eye for an eye’,- ‘tooth for a tooth’,- is the motto of?
questions.explanation: "Eye for an eye, tooth for a tooth" is the motto of the Retributive theory of punishment.

questions.title: The punishment theory which is supported by criminology is?
questions.explanation: The punishment theory supported by criminology is the Reformative theory.

questions.title: The aim of punishment is to educate the offender himself- this theory of punishment is called?
questions.explanation: The theory of punishment that aims to educate the offender himself is called the Reformative theory of punishment.

questions.title: Which theory justifies capital punishment?
questions.explanation: The Retributivism theory justifies capital punishment.

questions.title: The aim of_______theory of punishment is to prevent or deter others from committing similar offences.
questions.explanation: The aim of the Deterrent theory of punishment is to prevent or deter others from committing similar offenses.

questions.title: Aristotle divides justice into two; one is distributive justice and the other is?
questions.explanation: Aristotle divides justice into two: distributive justice and corrective justice.

questions.title: According to Aristotle______belongs to the rational soul.
questions.explanation: According to Aristotle, Intellectual virtue belongs to the rational soul.

questions.title: Who made the distinction between intellectual virtue and moral virtue?
questions.explanation: Aristotle made the distinction between intellectual virtue and moral virtue.

questions.title: "Virtue is the habit of choosing the relative mean". Who holds this view?
questions.explanation: Aristotle holds the view that "Virtue is the habit of choosing the relative mean."

questions.title: According to Plato_______is the special virtue of the traders.
questions.explanation: According to Plato, Temperance is the special virtue of the traders.

questions.title: According to Plato courage is the special virtue of the?
questions.explanation: According to Plato, courage is the special virtue of the Fighting class.
